title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: Set in the American South during the 1930s, this Pulitzer Prize-winning novel follows the story of Scout Finch and her father Atticus as he defends a black man accused of rape, exploring themes of racial injustice, moral growth, and the loss of innocence.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian social science fiction that follows Winston Smith as he rebels against the totalitarian regime of Oceania. The novel explores themes of surveillance, censorship, and the manipulation of truth.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A romantic novel following Elizabeth Bennet as she deals with issues of manners, upbringing, morality, education, and marriage in the society of the landed gentry of early 19th-century England.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: Set in the Jazz Age on Long Island, the novel depicts narrator Nick Carraway's interactions with mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and Gatsby's obsession to reunite with his former lover, Daisy Buchanan.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: The epic tale of Captain Ahab's obsessive quest for revenge against Moby Dick, the white whale that bit off his leg. A complex exploration of good and evil, obsession, and nature.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: An epic novel following five aristocratic families in Russia during the Napoleonic Era, exploring themes of love, war, marriage, and death.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: The story of 16-year-old Holden Caulfield's three days in New York City after being expelled from his boarding school, exploring themes of teenage alienation and loss of innocence.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: The adventures of home-loving hobbit Bilbo Baggins, who is convinced by the wizard Gandalf to join a group of dwarves in their quest to reclaim their mountain home from a dragon.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: Set in a futuristic World State, the novel anticipates developments in reproductive technology, sleep-learning, psychological manipulation, and classical conditioning that combine to change society.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A psychological novel that follows Rodion Raskolnikov, an impoverished ex-student in Saint Petersburg who formulates a plan to kill an unscrupulous pawnbroker for her money.

title: The Old Man and the Sea
author: Ernest Hemingway

title: Test Book
author: Test Author
description: A placeholder entry for testing purposes.
